convs.name: How Anchors Work

disagreements.detail: Production keeps the whole 0-5 anchor explanation as one chunk; candidate fragments it into 3 (0-1, 2-3, 4-5).

agreements: Full message range 0-5 is covered with no gaps or hallucinations.
Labels accurately track the anchor sub-topics.
convs.summary: A single coherent topic that production treats as one chunk is split into three. Labels are specific and correct, but the granularity is roughly triple the production reference.

convs.name: Divorce Rights in Mexico

disagreements.detail: Production has one divorce chunk (0-7); candidate fragments it into four (0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7).

agreements: Telenovela topic boundary at message 8 matches production exactly.
Final chunk 8-9 aligns with gold.
convs.summary: The candidate correctly isolates the telenovela tail at 8-9, matching the production split, but over-fragments the divorce-rights body into four chunks where production kept one. Labels are accurate.

convs.name: Fixing JavaScript ES6 Error

disagreements.detail: Chunk 18-19 end anchor is a spliced/chimera UUID (flagged endFlag chimera_prefix); a fabricated anchor is a real defect.
Candidate emits 15 chunks vs production's 11; production's 4-7, 8-11, 18-21, 22-25 blocks are each split into halves.

agreements: Topic ordering and command-by-command coverage closely tracks the granular production gold.
ETARGET (26-27) and ERESOLVE (28-29) chunks match production boundaries exactly.
convs.summary: Production is already fine-grained here and the candidate aligns well on several boundaries, but it splits four of production's two-message blocks and emits a hallucinated chimera anchor at chunk 18-19. Labels are descriptive though some misname npm as RPM.

convs.name: First Hold: Climbing Starting Point

disagreements.detail: Production keeps the entire 0-7 first-hold/challenge discussion as one chunk; candidate fragments it into four (0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7).

agreements: Full 0-7 range covered without gaps.
convs.summary: A single venue-booking topic that production treats as one chunk is quartered. The over-splitting is the dominant defect; labels are reasonable but the granularity is 4x the reference.

convs.name: Encoded String Verification

disagreements.detail: Production keeps the base64-decoding session as one chunk (0-8); candidate splits into three (0-3, 4-5, 6-8).

agreements: Full 0-8 range covered.
Labels stay on the base64 decoding topic.
convs.summary: One continuous base64-decoding topic is split three ways against production's single chunk. Coverage is complete and labels on-topic, but the fragmentation is the key disagreement.

convs.name: Mud versus clay differences

disagreements.detail: Candidate emits 23 chunks vs production's 13; nearly every production multi-message block (4-7, 8-13, 18-23, 24-27, 28-31, 38-45) is broken into two-message pieces.
Messages 42-43 left uncovered (gold covers the full 0-47 range).

agreements: Major topic transitions (clay->space chemistry at 14, diamond economics) are present.
Labels are specific and accurate per pair.
convs.summary: Production already chunks this long conversation into 13 spans; the candidate nearly doubles that to 23 by halving multi-message blocks, and drops messages 42-43. Labels are strong but granularity and a coverage gap pull boundary down.

convs.name: Comparing Microphone Audio

disagreements.detail: Production keeps 0-3 as one chunk; candidate splits into 0-1 (methodology) and 2-3 (software list).

agreements: Full 0-3 coverage.
Both labels are accurate and specific.
convs.summary: A short conversation that production treats as one chunk is split into a defensible methodology/software-list pair. Mild over-split against the production granularity but the split is coherent.

convs.name: Avoid Banding in DaVinci.

disagreements.detail: Production has 3 chunks (0-1, 2-7, 8-13); candidate emits 7, splitting the DaVinci block 2-7 into three and the nitroglycerin block 8-13 into three.

agreements: DaVinci->nitroglycerin topic shift at message 8 matches production exactly.
Opening banding chunk 0-1 matches production.
convs.summary: Candidate nails the two production topic boundaries (0-1 banding, then the shift to nitroglycerin at 8) but over-fragments both the DaVinci capabilities block and the chemistry block. Labels are accurate.

convs.name: Service Table Design for SIMS
agreements: Both chunk boundaries (0-1, 2-3) match production exactly.
Labels (service table design, SurrealQL inquiry) match production intent.
convs.summary: Exact boundary match with production: 0-1 schema design and 2-3 SurrealQL. Labels are specific and equivalent to the gold.

convs.name: File Size Creation Date

disagreements.detail: Production has 7 chunks; candidate emits 10, splitting production's 0-3 (Node fs), 8-11 (Arca-Swiss rig), and 12-15 (plate binding) blocks.

agreements: All major topic transitions (RAW formats, Arca-Swiss, MP4/MOV, DaVinci waveform) are present and ordered.
Labels are specific and accurate.
convs.summary: Coverage and topic ordering track production well, but several production multi-message blocks are halved, yielding 10 chunks vs 7. Labels are strong.

convs.name: New chat
agreements: Single chunk 0-4 matches production exactly.
Label (Spanish translation for photographic film) matches gold intent.
convs.summary: Perfect single-chunk match with production over the full 0-4 range, with an accurate label.

convs.name: Toyota's Kaizen Culture

disagreements.detail: Production keeps 0-3 as one chunk; candidate splits into 0-1 (Kaizen) and 2-3 (Lexus LFA).

agreements: Full 0-3 coverage.
Both labels accurate and tied to the Toyota theme.
convs.summary: Production treats this as one Toyota/Lexus chunk; candidate splits into a defensible Kaizen vs LFA pair. Mild over-split relative to production granularity.

convs.name: if you look at the articles in articles, you might see that they have a lot of c

disagreements.detail: Production keeps the whole citation-migration effort as one chunk (0-46); candidate fragments it into 12 chunks.
Trailing messages 47-48 (download Anthropic email attachment) uncovered; production captures this as a distinct second chunk.

agreements: Labels accurately narrate the iterative citation-migration steps.
convs.summary: Production sees two topics here (the 0-46 citation migration and the 47-48 attachment download); the candidate over-fragments the migration into 12 chunks AND misses the attachment tail entirely, so it both over-splits the main topic and drops the one real secondary topic.

convs.name: Comparing Mac computers for local AI performance
convs.summary: No production reference exists; excluded from scoring.

disagreements.detail: Production has 3 chunks (0-5 EDGE history, 6-9 iPhone/2003, 10-13 Wi-Fi); candidate emits 6, splitting each production block in two.

agreements: All chunk boundaries nest cleanly inside production's three spans.
Labels accurately track EDGE history, legacy-network support, and Wi-Fi timeline.
convs.summary: Candidate's six chunks subdivide each of production's three spans into halves; boundaries nest correctly and labels are specific, but the granularity is double the reference.

convs.name: SR-71s never downed

disagreements.detail: Production has 2 chunks (0-15 SR-71, 16-19 drifting); candidate splits the SR-71 body into five chunks.
Drifting chunks 16-19 and 18-19 overlap by 2 messages (flagged anomaly; policy allows <=1 boundary message).

agreements: SR-71 -> drifting topic shift around message 16 matches production.
Labels are accurate per span.
convs.summary: Candidate finds the correct high-level SR-71/drifting split but over-fragments the SR-71 block into five pieces and introduces a 2-message overlap in the drifting tail. Labels are good.

convs.name: Policy Document Database Import

disagreements.detail: Production has 5 chunks; candidate emits 6, splitting production's 0-3 DISCOVER step into 0-1 and 2-3.

agreements: Schema design, apply, and parse boundaries align with production's stepwise chunks.
Labels track the DISCOVER/SCHEMA/APPLY/PARSE workflow accurately.
convs.summary: Boundaries align closely with production's 5-chunk stepwise structure; the only divergence is splitting the discovery step in two. Labels are descriptive and accurate.

convs.name: WebSocket Message Queueing Fix

disagreements.detail: Production has 5 chunks (0-1, 2-3, 4-7, 8-10, 11-13); candidate emits 12 single-message chunks, atomizing nearly every message.
Trailing messages 12-13 (Deno rewrite tail) uncovered.

agreements: Labels accurately describe each WebSocket/Proxy/event step.
convs.summary: The candidate atomizes this into 12 one-message chunks against production's 5 multi-message spans and drops the 12-13 tail. Severe over-fragmentation plus a coverage gap; labels are accurate but the structure is far from the reference.

TeamViewer decline correctly isolated as the final chunk (8-9).

Labels are specific and accurate.
